APPEARANCES:
Paul Lannus of LANKFORD CRAWFORD MORENO & OSTERTAG LLP (925-300-3520), appearing for defendant / moving party Mack Trucks, Inc.

RULING:
DEFENDANT MACK TRUCKS, INC.'S MOTION BY DEFENDANT MACK TRUCKS, INC. TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS RESPONSES TO SAN FRANCISCO STANDARD ASBESTOS CASE INTERROGATORIES (LOSS OF CONSORTIUM), (WRONGFUL DEATH), AND (FRICTION); STANDARD DOCUMENT PRODUCTION REQUEST; AND REQUEST FOR SANCTION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant Mack Trucks, Inc.'s motion to compel plaintiffs' responses to San Francisco Standard Asbestos Case Interrogatories (loss of consortium, wrongful death, and friction) and standard document production request is denied as moot. Plaintiffs have served responses to the requested discovery. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $2120.00. Plaintiffs failed to serve timely responses to the discovery dictated by this court's Case Management Order and only served such responses after defendant filed this motion. (See CRC 3.1348.) Plaintiffs shall pay defendant in the amount of $2,120.00 within 15 days after this hearing. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Carrie Lin of COOLEY MANION JONES KUROWSKI LLP (415-512-4381), appearing for defendant / opposing party Hajoca Corporation

RULING:
DEFENDANT HAJOCA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hajoca Corp.'s general demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Although not cited by defendant, Bockrath is the controlling authority. Defendant's demurrer as to the successor-in-interest allegations is overruled. Plaintiffs' complaint pleads sufficient allegations pertaining to successor-in-interest liability. One order for both the demurrer and motion to strike was signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
